disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The whole right index finger is uniformly swollen from knuckle to tip (not just one joint), dusky and immobile
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe exactly what the finger looks like?
Is it the whole finger that's swollen, or just one knuckle?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's the whole index finger, all of it from the knuckle to the tip, puffed up uniformly like a sausage. It's a dusky purplish colour and I can't bend it at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The fingernails on the affected hand show multiple pits/dents
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed anything about your nails?
Are there any pits, dents or changes in the nails on that hand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — the nails on that hand have lots of little pits and dents in them. I've had that for a while.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really looked closely at my nails.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Two entirely distinct joint types on opposite sides flared at the same time — a small finger and a large weight-bearing knee (asymmetrical)
trigger_question_examples: Is it just the one joint, or more than one?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's two very different joints on opposite sides at once — my right index finger and my left knee. They both blew up together over the same day.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Longstanding psoriasis (flaky skin patches for years)
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any skin conditions?
Any history of psoriasis or skin patches?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I've had psoriasis for years — flaky patches that come and go.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No high spiking fever, no rigors/uncontrollable shaking, no racing heart or breathlessness (no systemic toxicity)
trigger_question_examples: Have you had a high fever, or teeth-chattering chills and shakes?
Do you feel your heart racing, or feel very unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel a bit warm and feverish but I'm not having shaking chills or teeth-chattering, my heart isn't racing and I'm not short of breath — mostly I'm just wiped out and in a lot of pain.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I feel a bit off but I haven't really measured anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The knee is warm, tight and full of fluid but the skin over it is not spreading fiery-red and it is not rapidly worsening hour to hour
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin over the knee going bright, spreading red?
Is the knee getting dramatically worse by the hour, or holding steady?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's warm and swollen and sore, but the skin isn't a spreading bright red and it's not getting massively worse every hour — it's been about the same bad since it came up.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's hard to tell, it just hurts.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe fatigue preceded the joint flare, and paracetamol gave zero relief
trigger_question_examples: Were you feeling unwell or tired before the joints flared?
Have you taken anything for the pain, and did it help?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'd been really wiped out and exhausted for a while before the joints flared up. I took paracetamol for the pain and it did absolutely nothing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone and cannot safely prepare food with the dominant hand and the knee both affected (self-care/safety concern)
trigger_question_examples: Do you have anyone at home to help you?
Are you able to manage day to day — cooking, getting around?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own and with my hand useless and the knee like this I can't safely cook or get around properly — I'm struggling to look after myself.
